top_signals.title: Middle East tensions rattle Asia markets after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran
top_signals.summary: Asia-Pacific markets fell Thursday while oil prices surged after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran heightened fears of a wider Middle East conflict.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: XRP drops 4% below $1.30 as heavy selling breaks key support zone
Expect investor activity in Premier League this summer, say Raine Group
top_signals.summary: Raine Group, the bank behind the sale of Chelsea and Sir Jim Ratcliffe’s minority purchase of Manchester United, has said to expect heightened activity this summer as Premier League clubs move to sell stakes and raise capital. The respected US merchant bank, which has recently advised on NBA Europe and franchise sales in the Hundred [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Curb on shareholder rights will hurt retail investors not activists, says Oasis
top_signals.summary: The government and LDP suggest limiting shareholder proposals to those holding at least 1% of voting rights, up from the current threshold of 300.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Japan plans ‘bridging bonds’ to fund investment initiatives
top_signals.summary: Bridging bonds are designed to cover temporary funding shortfalls on the premise that future funding resources can be secured.
